item.text: I’ll inspect the repository instructions and existing files, then derive the reachability compression, implement `solution.py`, and validate it with samples plus randomized brute-force comparisons.

The key compression is now clear: with `A < B`, any nonzero reachability state becomes “all of the last B squares are reachable” after at most `A(A−1)+2B` consecutive good squares. Bad runs only need `B` steps—`B` consecutive bad squares erase all reachability. The `A = B` case is a fixed arithmetic progression and is handled directly.

item.text:
Implemented the complete solution in [solution.py](/Users/james/Library/Mobile%20Documents/com~apple~CloudDocs/Code/live-code-bench/LiveCodeBench/native-codex-gpt-5.6-sol-ultrafast-high-q1-5-2026-07-13_145017/q3_abc388_f/solution.py).

Validated with:

- All three sample cases
- Randomized brute-force comparisons
- Exhaustive reachability-state checks for `B ≤ 15`
- Python syntax compilation

Complexity: `O(M · (A² + B))` time and `O(M)` memory.
